howItWorks.intro: This fantasy football points calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Passing: 1 pt per 25 yards, 4 pts per TD, -2 per INT
Rushing: 1 pt per 10 yards, 6 pts per TD
Receiving: 1 pt per 10 yards, 6 pts per TD
PPR: +1 pt per reception, Half-PPR: +0.5 per reception
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is PPR scoring?
faq.answer: PPR (Points Per Reception) awards 1 point for each catch. Half-PPR awards 0.5 per catch. This boosts the value of pass-catching running backs and slot receivers compared to standard scoring.

faq.question: How many fantasy points is a good game?
faq.answer: For QBs: 20+ is good, 30+ is great. For RBs/WRs: 15+ is good in standard, 20+ in PPR. A player averaging 15+ points per week in PPR is typically a reliable fantasy starter.

faq.question: Why do passing TDs only give 4 points?
faq.answer: Passing TDs award 4 points (vs 6 for rushing/receiving TDs) because QBs accumulate yards and TDs at a much higher rate. This balances QB scoring with other positions to maintain competitive leagues.

faq.question: Is the Fantasy Football Points Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Fantasy Football Points Calculator?
sections.body: The Fantasy Football Points Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your passing yards, passing tds, interceptions, you get instant results including standard points, half-ppr points, full ppr points. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting standard points right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your passing yards and need to find the right standard points. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Passing Yards
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your passing yards — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: passing tds, interceptions, rushing yards, rushing tds, receptions, receiving yards, receiving tds. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your standard points, half-ppr points, full ppr points. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your passing yards before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both standard points and half-ppr points — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to analyze performance.
If you're unsure about your receiving tds, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
